text: In Ecuador, community nurses serve as the backbone of health promotion and disease prevention, particularly within rural and marginalized urban settings. This study examines the critical contributions of these professionals in advancing health education, spearheading vaccination campaigns, and managing chronic conditions. Findings indicate that targeted educational interventions significantly foster healthier lifestyles and reduce the incidence of gastrointestinal diseases and childhood malnutrition. Furthermore, nursing-led immunization efforts have successfully expanded coverage in underserved regions, while dedicated follow-up programs have enhanced treatment adherence and overall patient quality of life. Despite these successes, the field faces substantial hurdles, including dilapidated infrastructure, resource scarcity, and limited access to professional development. To optimize the impact of community nursing, this paper advocates for the development of robust public policies, the integration of innovative healthcare technologies, and the fostering of cross-sectoral community partnerships. Addressing these systemic challenges is essential to strengthening the nursing workforce and advancing public health outcomes across Ecuador.
